story: 107.R1 follow-up: toke repair sample-50 (1 Opus attempt each)

programs.hint: Undeclared identifier 'std'. Check spelling; ensure it is bound with let before use; verify imports. Did you mean 'str'?
programs.source: m=cfgsec;f=checkdebug(cfg:$str):$bool{let hasdebug=cfg.contains("debug");if(hasdebug){let debugval=cfg.get("debug");if(debugval="true"){<true};};<false};f=checkcreds(cfg:$str):$bool{let hasuser=cfg.contains("user");let haspass=cfg.contains("pass");if(hasuser){if(haspass){let userval=cfg.get("user");let passval=cfg.get("pass");if(userval="admin"){if(passval="password"){<true};};};};<false};f=checkweak(cfg:$str):$bool{let haslevel=cfg.contains("level");if(haslevel){let levelval=cfg.get("level");if(levelval="low"){<true};};<false};f=main():$i64{let cfg=std.stdin.read();let hasdebug=checkdebug(cfg);let hascreds=checkcreds(cfg);let hasweak=checkweak(cfg);let found=mut.false;if(hasdebug){found=true};if(hascreds){found=true};if(hasweak){found=true};if(found){<1};<0};

programs.hint: Code is incomplete — truncated or an unclosed (/[/{/". Count opening vs closing delimiters; if the program looks heavily truncated, rewrite the complete program from scratch rather than trying to patch the end.

Missing semicolon. Toke separates EVERY statement with ; (never newlines). Every let/assignment/call/return needs a trailing ;. Inside lp(){body}, body statements also need ; between them. | Extra closing paren ')'. Count parens — every '(' needs exactly one ')'. Common cause: doubled if((cond)) or trailing paren on a call.
programs.source: m=swap;f=swap(x:$i64;y:$i64):$i64{let fee=mut.0;let amount=mut.0;if(x<0){<0};if(y<0){<0};let product=x*y;let target=product/(100 as$i64));let diff=target-product/100;let swapval=mut.0;if(diff>0){swapval=x-diff}el{swapval=x+diff};<swapval};f=main():$i64{<swap(100;50))};

programs.source: m=hashcheck;f=hashcheck(p:$str;q:$str):$str{let res=mut."INVALID";let plen=p.len;let qlen=q.len;if(plen>0){if(qlen>19){let first=q.substr(0;19);let rest=q.substr(19;qlen-19));let check=s.concat(first;p);let valid=mut.false;if(check.len=rest.len){let i=mut.0;lp(let idx=0;idx<check.len;idx=idx+1){let c1=check.get(idx);let c2=rest.get(idx);if(!(c1=c2)){i=idx;br};};if(i=check.len){valid=true};};if(!(valid=true)){res="INVALID"}el{res="VALID"}};};<res};};f=main():$void{let p=io:std.io.readline();let q=io:std.io.readline();hashcheck(p;q);};

programs.hint: 'el' must directly follow an if(...){...} block: if(cond){body}el{body}. Cannot stand alone.
programs.source: m=gpatrend;f=parsefloat(s:$str):$f64{let num=mut.0.0;let i=mut.0;let len=s.len;lp(let j=0;j<len;j=j+1){let ch=s.get(j);if(ch="."){i=j+1;lp(let k=i;k<len;k=k+1){let dig=s.get(k);if(!(dig>47))el{if(!(dig<58))){i=k+1;}};};}el{if(!(ch>47))){if(!(ch<58))){let dig=ch as$f64-48.0;num=num+dig;};};};};};};};};};};};};};};};};};};};};};};};};}

programs.hint: Missing semicolon. Toke separates EVERY statement with ; (never newlines). Every let/assignment/call/return needs a trailing ;. Inside lp(){body}, body statements also need ; between them. | Unexpected token in expression. Common causes: (a) keyword used as identifier (i, f, t, m, as, el, lp, br, let, mut, rt, mt) — rename; (b) missing operator between values; (c) comma instead of semicolon (toke uses ; everywhere). | Code is incomplete — truncated or an unclosed (/[/{/".
